nodes.context: Mafia-type organization in Calabria, Southern Italy.
Collegial body of the 'Ndrangheta, composed of leading members to decide important questions and settle disputes.
nodes.time_period: established at the end of the Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991)

nodes.context: Mafia organization in Sicily, contributed to ending the Second 'Ndrangheta war and suggested setting up a superordinate body.
Group of 'Ndrangheta families in Southern Ontario, Canada, including Coluccio, Tavernese, DeMaria, Figliomeni, Ruso, and Commisso crime families.

edges.evidence_quote: The Camera di Controllo (Board of Control), is a collegial body of the 'Ndrangheta
edges.context: Camera di Controllo is a governing body within the 'Ndrangheta organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sicilian Mafia contributed to the end of the conflict and probably suggested the subsequent set up of a superordinate body, similar to the Sicilian Mafia Commission
edges.context: Sicilian Mafia helped end the Second 'Ndrangheta war and advised creating the Camera di Controllo.

edges.evidence_quote: The body, also referred to as the Commission in reference to its Sicilian counterpart
edges.context: Camera di Controllo is modeled after the Sicilian Mafia Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: A Camera di Controllo also exists in Canada, according to 'confidential information' of the police 'which they believe to be reliable.' It consists of six or seven Toronto-area men, who co-ordinate activities and resolves disputes among the Siderno Group in Southern Ontario.
edges.context: The Canadian Camera di Controllo coordinates activities and resolves disputes for the Siderno Group.
